And the Lord spoke unto Moses and Aaron in the land of Egypt, saying (Exod. 12:1). Is it not a fact that He spoke only to Moses? Why, then, does the Scripture say unto Moses and Aaron? To indicate that just as Moses was included in the revelation, so too was Aaron. Where do we learn that He did not speak to Aaron? matches.x: you shall eat unleavened bread. The exact significance here of matzah is unclear. The Bible presents unleavened bread as both a festive food (Genesis 19:3) and a symbol of oppression, associating “the bread of distress” with the memory of the Exodus (Deuteronomy 16:3).
